entries.answer_stated: f(x) = x + c (x > 0) for a constant c >= 0
entries.justification: Complete and rigorous. The hypothesis is repackaged as |(f(x)+y)^2 - (x+f(y))^2| <= (x - f(y))^2, giving the functional equation and AP orbits with p = f - id >= 0. A verified descent lemma (|c-e|(4t-c+e) <= (c-e)^2 forces e = c since 4t-c+e > |c-e| when t > c) extends any c-orbit backwards to a seed r in (0, c]; if a second value d < c existed, the left inequality at (x_n, y) is Q(x_n) = (x_n - (c+y))^2 - s^2 >= 0 with s^2 = 2(c-d)(c+d+2y) (identity verified), and after enlarging y so 2s > c the c-step orbit starting at r <= c cannot jump the forbidden interval of length 2s - contradiction. Sufficiency via QM-AM-GM since f(x)+y = x+f(y). No gaps found.

entries.answer_stated: f(x) = x + c for a constant c >= 0
entries.justification: Complete and rigorous, via an asymptotic-sandwich route distinct from the orbit-interleaving proofs. x = f(y) collides the two squared inequalities into f(f(y)) = 2f(y) - y, so orbits are APs and g = f - id >= 0. Given g(a) = d > 0, picking the orbit point y = a + nd with |f(y) - x| <= d/2 and the two elementary estimates E1/E2 (I verified both expansions: (1+u) - (1 + u/2 - u^2/2)^2 = -(u^2/4)(u-3)(u+1) >= 0 on [-1,3], and 2x^2 + 2(x+eps)^2 = (2x+eps)^2 + eps^2) yield the two-sided pinch d - d^2/(4x) <= g(x) <= d + d^2/(16x-4d) for x >= a+d, whence all positive gaps are equal and g = d on a half-line; the downward bootstrap with x = y + d makes both squared inequalities pin f(y) = y + d exactly, and induction in steps of d covers all of R_{>0}. Sufficiency is exactly QM-AM-GM on the pair (x, y+c), and c >= 0 is automatic since c is a gap value. Every squaring/positivity step is justified (x > d, 2x + eps > 0, epsilon bounds all checked); no gaps found.

entries.answer_stated: f(x) = x + c for all x > 0, c >= 0
entries.justification: Complete and rigorous via an analytic route distinct from the orbit-interleaving proofs. After the functional equation and g >= 0, applying the chain at (f(u), v) with f(f(u)) = 2f(u) - u yields |g(u)-g(v)| <= (sqrt(f(u)) - sqrt(f(v)))^2 (verified). This forces f strictly increasing (the Delta <= 0 cases each contradict the estimate), hence g nondecreasing via f^n(u) > f^n(v) with n -> infty, then two-sided continuity of g (jump J satisfies J <= (sqrt(A+J)-sqrt(A))^2 < J), and finally a Riemann-sum telescoping over an equipartition with sum Delta_i^2 <= max Delta_i * (f(b)-f(a)) -> 0 by uniform continuity, so g is constant. Verification is standard QM-AM-GM. Only microscopic unstated details (e.g. f(t) - J >= t > 0 in the left-limit step), well within '7 with minor blemishes'.

entries.answer_stated: f(x) = x + c for every x > 0, c >= 0
entries.justification: Complete and rigorous, via the orbit-interleaving + connectedness route (distinct from this model's default-effort analytic route, and closer to gpt-5.6-sol's). x = f(y) collapses both outer expressions to f(y), giving f(f(y)) = 2f(y) - y, AP orbits, and d >= 0. Two positive displacements a > b are killed by floor-alignment: m_n = floor((u+na-v-b)/b) gives 0 <= p_n - q_n < b (verified), and the first inequality at (p_n, v + m_n b) yields a - b <= (p_n - q_n)^2 / (sqrt(2(p_n^2+q_n^2)) + p_n + q_n) < b^2/(denominator -> infinity) - the algebraic identity 2(p^2+q^2) - (p+q)^2 = (p-q)^2 checks. Mixed case {0, c}: both the zero-set and c-set are shown open via the first inequality on approaching sequences (limit 2L + c <= 2L, impossible), and connectedness of R_{>0} with B nonempty forces d = c everywhere. Sufficiency is exactly RMS-AM-GM on (x, y+c), and c >= 0 is automatic from d >= 0. Every step verified; no gaps.

entries.answer_stated: f(x) = x + c for a constant c >= 0
entries.justification: Complete and rigorous; independently verified end to end (all algebraic identities checked with a CAS). Squaring the two-sided hypothesis gives alpha = (f(x)+y)^2 - 4x f(y) >= 0 and beta = 2x^2 + 2f(y)^2 - (f(x)+y)^2 >= 0 with alpha + beta = 2(x - f(y))^2. Setting x = f(y) forces alpha = beta = 0, hence the core identity f(f(y)) = 2f(y) - y and c(f(y)) = c(y) where c = f - id. Iterating yields AP orbits f^n(y) = y + n c(y); positivity of f forbids c(y) < 0, so c >= 0 and f >= id. The confinement bound |c(x) - c(y)| <= (x - P)^2 / P with P = f(y) is correctly derived from both squared inequalities via I_L = -(sqrt x - sqrt P)^2 and I_U = (x-P)^2/(sqrt(2x^2+2P^2)+x+P), each bounded by (x-P)^2/P. Interleaving two positive-gap orbits: choose B_m within q/2 of the image point P_n = a + np -> infinity, giving |p - q| <= (q/2)^2/P_n -> 0, so all positive c-values coincide. The fixed-point/positive coexistence case (the a=0-type case other models mishandled) is handled cleanly: the B-inequality at (a in Q, b in Z) gives (a-b)^2 >= p^2 + 2p(a+b) >= p^2, hence dist(Q,Z) >= p > 0, so Z and Q are both open, contradicting connectedness of (0,infinity); one is empty and c is constant. Sufficiency is exactly QM-AM-GM on (x, y+c) with the (x-y-c)^2 >= 0 identity, and c >= 0 is forced. No gaps found.

entries.answer_stated: f(x) = x (claims identity is the only solution -- incorrect/incomplete, misses the family f(x)=x+c)
entries.justification: Empty proof (the Full proof section reads '(empty)') and an incorrect conjectured answer. Grok did locate the genuine entry point: the substitution x = f(y) collapses both outer terms to f(y) and forces (f(x)+y)/2 = f(y), i.e. the structural identity f(f(y)) = 2f(y) - y (stated as y = 2a - f(a) on preimages). It also correctly ruled out f(x) = cx except c = 1, and constants. But it never discovered the additive family: its stated aim is 'prove f = id is the only solution', so the claimed answer f(x) = x is wrong and incomplete (missing every c > 0). None of the substantive steps are carried out -- no f >= id, no AP orbits, no nonnegativity of c, no constancy argument, no coexistence handling, and no sufficiency direction. Credit only for identifying the crux substitution and the image-level identity; otherwise no proof and a wrong final characterization.
key_issues: Conjectured answer f(x)=x is wrong: misses the entire family f(x)=x+c for c>0
Core identity f(f(y))=2f(y)-y found only implicitly on the image; no monotonicity/orbit/constancy argument built on it
No sufficiency direction, no nonnegativity/constancy proof
